name: workable
description: Access Workable recruiting data — jobs, candidates, and pipeline stages — and add comments or move candidates

envVars.name: WORKABLE_API_TOKEN
envVars.description: Workable API access token, generated under Settings → Integrations → Apps → Generate new token

envVars.name: WORKABLE_SUBDOMAIN
envVars.description: Your Workable account subdomain ({subdomain}.workable.com), shown in your company profile settings

tools.name: List Jobs
tools.description: List jobs in the Workable account with optional state filtering

subdomain.description: Workable account subdomain (the {subdomain} in {subdomain}.workable.com; see WORKABLE_SUBDOMAIN)

state.description: Filter by job state: draft, published, archived, or closed

limit.description: Number of jobs to return per page (max 100)

since_id.description: Pagination: only return jobs with ID greater than or equal to this ID

created_after.description: Only jobs created after this ISO 8601 timestamp

updated_after.description: Only jobs updated after this ISO 8601 timestamp

include_fields.description: Extra fields to include, e.g. description, full_description, requirements, benefits

historicalSummary.omitted: job descriptions, location details, and provider-specific payload fields

tools.name: Get Job
tools.description: Get the full details of a job by its shortcode

shortcode.description: The job's shortcode, e.g. GROOV005 (from List Jobs)

tools.name: List Candidates
tools.description: List candidates account-wide or for a specific job (filter by job shortcode, stage, or email)

shortcode.description: Only return candidates for the job with this shortcode

stage.description: Only return candidates in this stage (stage slug, e.g. sourced, applied, interview)

email.description: Only return candidates with this email address

limit.description: Number of candidates to return per page (max 100)

since_id.description: Pagination: only return candidates with ID greater than or equal to this ID

created_after.description: Only candidates created after this ISO 8601 timestamp

updated_after.description: Only candidates updated after this ISO 8601 timestamp

historicalSummary.omitted: candidate profiles, resumes, and provider-specific payload fields

tools.name: Get Candidate
tools.description: Get the full profile of a candidate by ID, including stage, answers, and attachments

tools.name: List Members
tools.description: List account members to find the member IDs required by the comment and move-candidate tools

role.description: Filter by recruiting role: simple, admin, or reviewer

shortcode.description: Filter for a specific job shortcode; returns only that job's collaborators

limit.description: Number of members to return per page

since_id.description: Pagination: only return members with ID greater than or equal to this ID

tools.name: List Stages
tools.description: List the recruitment pipeline stages to find stage slugs for candidate filtering and moves

tools.name: Create Candidate
tools.description: Add a candidate to a job, either as sourced or as having applied

shortcode.description: Shortcode of the job to add the candidate to (from List Jobs)

sourced.description: Whether the candidate was sourced (true, default) or applied themselves (false); determines the default starting stage

stage.description: Stage slug to place the candidate in, overriding the sourced/applied default (see List Stages)

tools.name: Create Candidate Comment
tools.description: Post a comment on a candidate's timeline on behalf of a team member

member_id.description: ID of the team member creating the comment (from the account's members)

comment.description: Comment object: { "body": "comment text" } with optional "policy" array restricting visibility to roles, e.g. ["recruiter", "admin"]

tools.name: Move Candidate
tools.description: Move a candidate to another pipeline stage on behalf of a team member

member_id.description: ID of the team member performing the move

target_stage.description: Slug of the stage to move the candidate to, e.g. interview or offer

prompts.title: Open roles overview
prompts.prompt: List my published jobs in Workable and summarize how many candidates are in each pipeline.

prompts.title: Review a hiring stage
prompts.prompt: Show me the candidates currently in the interview stage for a job I specify and summarize their profiles.

prompts.title: Comment on a candidate
prompts.prompt: Look up a candidate I specify in Workable and add a comment to their timeline with my feedback.

SETUP_GUIDE.title: Workable API setup

steps.title: Get a Workable account
steps.description: Sign up at https://www.workable.com — a 15-day free trial account is enough for testing the API against your own data.

steps.title: Generate an access token
steps.description: In Workable, open the top-right dropdown menu → Integrations, then click 'Generate new token'. Select the scopes you need (r_jobs and r_candidates for reads; w_candidates for comments and stage moves). The token is shown only once — copy it immediately.

steps.title: Find your subdomain
steps.description: Your account subdomain is the {subdomain} part of {subdomain}.workable.com and is shown on your company profile settings page.

steps.title: Store the credentials
steps.description: Add WORKABLE_API_TOKEN=<your token> and WORKABLE_SUBDOMAIN=<your subdomain> to your .env file. Requests go to https://{subdomain}.workable.com/spi/v3 with an Authorization: Bearer header.

notes: Tokens are scoped at generation time — writes need w_candidates (comments also accept w_comments)
Revoking a token breaks every script using it; generate a new one and update all consumers
List endpoints paginate via the paging.next URL and since_id cursors (max 100 per page)
